name: Garuda Vahana
description: Garuda Vahana is not a physical item in the traditional sense, but rather refers to Garuda, the divine eagle-like being who serves as the mount (Vahana) of the Hindu god Vishnu. Garuda is a powerful and revered figure in his own right, known for his immense strength, speed, and unwavering devotion to Vishnu. He is often depicted as a majestic creature with a golden body, large wings, and a fierce gaze. As Vishnu's Vahana, Garuda carries him across the cosmos, symbolizing the divine power and sovereignty of Vishnu. Garuda's origins are rooted in the story of his birth, where he hatched from an egg after a long period of incubation, emerging with blazing brilliance. He is the son of Vinata and Sage Kashyapa. His primary power lies in his ability to traverse vast distances with incredible speed and his immunity to poison. He also possesses immense strength, capable of battling even the most formidable demons and serpents. Garuda's significance extends beyond being a mere vehicle; he is a protector, a symbol of courage, and a destroyer of evil. He is often invoked for protection from harm and is considered a powerful ally to devotees of Vishnu. The concept of Garuda Vahana represents the inseparable bond between the deity and his mount, highlighting the divine power and grace that permeates the universe.
subtitle: Vishnu's Divine Eagle Mount
origin: Born from the egg of Vinata and Sage Kashyapa
currentLocation: Vaikuntha (Vishnu's abode) and throughout the cosmos

keyMyths.title: Garuda's Birth
keyMyths.description: Garuda hatched from an egg after a long incubation period, emerging as a powerful and radiant being, signifying his divine origins and immense potential.

keyMyths.title: Garuda and the Amrita
keyMyths.description: Garuda undertook a perilous journey to retrieve the Amrita (elixir of immortality) from Indra to free his mother from slavery, showcasing his strength and devotion.

sources.author: Vyasadeva
sources.description: A comprehensive epic that details Garuda's birth, adventures, and role.
sources.text: A comprehensive epic that details Garuda's birth, adventures, and role.
